statement: Operator/debug/reporting endpoints form a separate reliability class with predictable read amplification that should be optimized independently of core run progression logic.
rationale: Insights repeatedly flag lab summary/list endpoints, getStuckWork, and autoHealProcess for read-limit pressure while bounded per-run health paths remained usable for live triage.
keyAssumptions: Core orchestration can remain healthy even when heavy operator endpoints degrade
validationCriteria: Operator dashboards and stuck-work queries remain within limits under active load after bounded-query redesign
No regression to core run throughput when operator surfaces are exercised
critiquePoints: Operator and core paths still share underlying tables; strict isolation is not absolute
questions: What are the minimal per-view aggregates needed to replace expensive scans?
